verification_note: Batch 026 starts the verified-folklore approach. Each included Nilalang has an external folklore reference URL and ALAMAT-specific lore is clearly adapted for gameplay.

monsters.passive_ability: Gem-Horn Instinct — Gains Luck and Speed when carrying beadwork, gems, or metal charms. Enemy treasure-steal effects have reduced accuracy against Anggitay.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Jeweled Horn Skewer — Charges one enemy with a glowing horn strike, dealing Beast/Metal damage and applying Marked.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Centauride Gem Trail — Gallops across the field leaving bright gem-lights that grant Haste to allies and Blind enemies who chase her.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Guard, Haste, Spirit Ward, Counter Ready, Reveal, Barrier, Calm, Blessing, Fear Cry, Quick Strike, Protect Ally, Fortify, Cleanse, Mark Prey, Regeneration
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires returning stolen jewelry and refusing to exploit her horn
monsters.habitat: Balete Grove, Bamboo Thicket, Mountain Peak, Market Town outskirts, Moonlit Clearing
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Polished bead, forest fruit, clean water, flower garland, unclaimed gemstone
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Anggitay is remembered as a female centaur-like being: human above the waist and horse below, sometimes bearing a single horn and an attraction to gems or jewelry. In ALAMAT, Anggitay are not simple treasure-hoarders. They are keepers of bright objects that carry memory—wedding beads, heirloom earrings, river stones, and lost offerings. This Anggitay appears when a merchant steals jewelry from a shrine and blames forest spirits for the disappearance. To earn her trust, the player must recover the stolen ornaments, identify which objects are sacred and which are merely decorative, and refuse the temptation to cut her horn for power. Once bonded, Anggitay becomes a fast ranger who excels at marked targets, evasion, and luck-based support.
monsters.folklore_summary: Anggitay is described in Philippine folklore as a female centaur-like creature with a woman's upper body and a horse's lower body. Some accounts give her a single horn and link her to gemstones or jewelry.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Anggitay as a forest hybrid guardian of bright heirlooms and sacred ornaments, dangerous mainly to thieves and exploiters.
monsters.physical_description: A graceful centauride with warm brown skin, long dark hair, a mare's lower body, polished hoof ornaments, beadwork across the chest, and one luminous horn like moonlit pearl. Gem sparks trail behind her gallop.
monsters.weaknesses: Greed traps, broken trust, grounding Earth seals, anti-beast snares, corrupted gemstones
monsters.resistances: Forest, Beast, Blind, Marked backlash, theft effects, speed reduction
monsters.npc_related: Bai Amirah the bead artisan; Mang Pilo the dishonest merchant; Apo Marikit the shrine caretaker
monsters.boss_variant: Anggitay ng Pitong Hiyas — uses seven gem-trails with different field effects.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Anggitay ng Ninakaw na Hiyas — becomes obsessed with cursed jewels and blinds allies and enemies alike.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Banal na Palamuti — radiant horned guardian of heirloom offerings.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A bright horse-like call mixed with bead chimes and a proud human laugh.
monsters.drop_items: Gem-Horn Dust, Beadwork Thread, Moon Hoof Shard, Forest Jewel
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Beadwork, Bell, Anting-Anting, Relic Weapon, Woven Garb
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Anggitay inspired by Philippine folklore: female centauride, human upper body, mare lower body, single luminous horn, beadwork ornaments, dark flowing hair, gem-light hoof trail, transparent background, readable chibi top-down RPG ranger silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Anggitay, Filipino female centaur-like being from Batangas folklore, one pearl-like horn, beadwork, horse body implied, forest shrine gemstones, proud protective expression, painterly RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Anggitay lowers her luminous horn, gallops in a crescent path, gem-lights burst behind her hooves, one enemy is marked by a jewel glyph, and allies receive a speed shimmer.
monsters.design_notes: Verified folklore basis. Avoid treating her as generic unicorn-centaur; emphasize Batangas origin, jewel memory, and anti-exploitation trust quest.

monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Ibanag folklore; Philippine minotaur-like creature traditions
monsters.passive_ability: Ember Jewel Guard — Gains Defense when taunted or when an enemy attempts to steal, dispel, or break an item-related buff.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Smoke-Nostril Charge — Charges one enemy with bull force, dealing Beast/Earth damage and applying Taunted.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Jewel of Tenfold Strength — The ear-jewel glows like an ember, granting Fortified to Sarangay and increasing allied Attack for two turns.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Guard, Haste, Spirit Ward, Counter Ready, Reveal, Barrier, Calm, Blessing, Fear Cry, Quick Strike, Protect Ally, Fortify, Cleanse, Mark Prey, Regeneration
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires proving you will not steal the ear-jewel
monsters.habitat: Mountain Peak, Limestone Cave, Highland Village, Rice Terraces, Spirit Threshold
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Mountain salt, polished wooden ring, roasted root crops, unclaimed gem, honest oath
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Sarangay is often described as a bull-man or water-buffalo-like humanoid who guards a powerful jewel attached to its ear. In ALAMAT, every Sarangay is defined by a test of greed. Its jewel is not merely treasure; it is a living ember of strength entrusted to the creature by old mountain spirits. This Sarangay blocks a pass after treasure hunters cut the ears from carved guardian statues, believing one hides the real gem. To earn trust, the player must return the stolen statue pieces, defeat the hunters without taking their loot, and stand before the Sarangay's jewel without reaching for it. Once bonded, Sarangay becomes a durable tank who punishes theft, protects allies, and channels jewel-fire into physical strength.
monsters.folklore_summary: Sarangay is a Philippine minotaur-like creature often described as half-man and half-bull or water buffalo. Accounts describe a jewel attached to its ears, which it guards fiercely.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT frames Sarangay as a jewel guardian whose rage tests whether humans value power more than trust.
monsters.physical_description: A towering bull-man with dark coarse hair, heavy horns, muscular arms, wooden ear rings, smoke curling from the nostrils, and a glowing ember-like jewel fixed near one ear.
monsters.weaknesses: Trickster theft bait, calm oath rites, water over ember jewel, broken guardian statues, anti-beast snares
monsters.resistances: Beast, Earth, Taunt, Fear, physical knockback, item-steal effects
monsters.npc_related: Apo Raniag the mountain elder; Berto the treasure hunter; Mayang the pass guide
monsters.boss_variant: Sarangay ng Nagbabagang Hiyas — jewel pulses with defensive phases and counter-charges.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Sarangay ng Sakim na Hiyas — jewel turns black and drives endless possessive rage.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Hiyas na Tapat — radiant bull-guardian who lends strength only to the honorable.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A deep bull bellow followed by smoke bursts and a ringing jewel pulse.
monsters.drop_items: Ember Jewel Dust, Wooden Ear Ring, Bull-Hair Cord, Mountain Horn Chip
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Relic Weapon, Totem, Bell, Anting-Anting, Beadwork
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Sarangay inspired by Philippine folklore: towering bull-man or water-buffalo humanoid, dark coarse hair, heavy horns, wooden ear rings, ember jewel near ear, smoke from nostrils, transparent background, readable chibi RPG tank silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Sarangay, Philippine bull-man jewel guardian, heavy horns, dark hair, glowing ember jewel at ear, smoke from nostrils, mountain pass background, powerful RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Sarangay exhales smoke, the ear-jewel glows red, it scrapes the ground with one hoof, charges forward, then jewel light expands into a defensive aura.
monsters.design_notes: Verified folklore basis. Do not make it just a minotaur; highlight ear-jewel, Ibanag link, and anti-greed trial.

monsters.passive_ability: Do Not Whistle Back — Enemies using sound skills against Mantiw suffer reduced accuracy and may become Taunted.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Palm-Top Whistle — Releases a huge whistling gust that pushes one enemy back and applies Slow.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Thirty-Foot Lift — Gently but firmly lifts enemies into a high palm illusion, applying Fear and removing Haste.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Guard, Haste, Spirit Ward, Counter Ready, Reveal, Barrier, Calm, Blessing, Fear Cry, Quick Strike, Protect Ally, Fortify, Cleanse, Mark Prey, Regeneration
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires respecting the giant's whistle and helping restore field boundaries
monsters.habitat: Rice Terraces, Highland Village, Bamboo Thicket, Mountain Peak, Balete Grove
monsters.weather_preference: Clear, Mist, Sea Breeze, Spirit Glow
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Buri palm leaf, coconut water, field rice, quiet song, boundary marker
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Mantiw are Western Visayan giants associated with fields, palms, and whistling. They are often described as peaceful but easily offended when humans mock or imitate their whistle. In ALAMAT, Mantiw are field-bound spirits who mark the borders between farms, palms, and old paths. This Mantiw begins carrying rude travelers to the top of the tallest palm after a group of youths turns its ancient whistle into a mocking festival game. To earn trust, the player must restore field boundary stones, apologize through silence rather than noise, and help a trapped traveler climb down safely. Once bonded, Mantiw becomes a tall guardian who controls sound, wind, fear, and forced movement without unnecessary cruelty.
monsters.folklore_summary: Mantiw are described in Iloilo/Panay folklore as very tall, often peaceful spirits seen in fields or near coconut and buri palms. They are known for whistling and may punish humans who whistle along disrespectfully.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Mantiw as a field giant whose whistle is sacred boundary language, not entertainment for mockery.
monsters.physical_description: A towering fair-skinned giant with broad shoulders, long limbs, hooked nose, woven field cloth, palm-leaf sash, and a gentle but intimidating expression. Wind rings appear around its mouth when it whistles.
monsters.weaknesses: Mockery, broken field markers, silence-binding charms, corrupted palm groves, lightning overexposure
monsters.resistances: Mountain, Forest, Sound, Fear, knockback, Taunt
monsters.npc_related: Mang Pilo the field owner; Tala the trapped youth; Apo Luntian the boundary elder
monsters.boss_variant: Mantiw ng Tatlong Palma — uses three palm illusions and whistle phases.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Mantiw ng Mapait na Sipol — turns sacred whistle into panic and Despair.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Tahimik na Hangganan — gentle field giant whose whistle guides travelers home.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A clear high whistle from far above, followed by a giant's slow field-step.
monsters.drop_items: Palm Whistle Reed, Giant Field Thread, Buri Leaf Charm, Boundary Stone Chip
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Instrument, Totem, Woven Garb, Bell, Anting-Anting
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Mantiw inspired by Western Visayan folklore: thirty-foot field giant, broad shoulders, fair complexion, hooked nose, palm-leaf sash, woven field cloth, wind rings from whistle, transparent background, readable chibi RPG guardian silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Mantiw, Iloilo/Panay whistling field giant, tall peaceful spirit leaning near coconut and buri palms, wind rings around mouth, dusk rice-field background, respectful RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Mantiw leans over the battlefield, whistles once, palm leaves bend into wind glyphs, an enemy is lifted into a tall palm illusion, and allies gain protective field wind.
monsters.design_notes: Verified folklore basis. Avoid crude giant tropes; emphasize peaceful dignity and taboo around imitating the whistle.

monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Pangasinan ghost stories and World War II-era haunting traditions
monsters.passive_ability: Reed-Mat Haunting — Gains Spirit Defense in Fog or Graveyard habitats. Enemies affected by Fear or Despair take extra Spirit Leak damage.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Mat That Blocks the Road — A spectral reed mat appears before one enemy, applying Root and Fear.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Unburied War Echo — Releases a wave of old battlefield grief, applying Despair to all enemies and cleansing one ally's Fear.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Guard, Haste, Spirit Ward, Counter Ready, Reveal, Barrier, Calm, Blessing, Fear Cry, Quick Strike, Protect Ally, Fortify, Cleanse, Mark Prey, Regeneration
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires identifying the dead, opening the reed mat respectfully, and completing burial rites
monsters.habitat: Graveyard, Spanish-Era Road, Ancestral House, Spirit Threshold, Bamboo Thicket
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Reed mat strip, white candle, name ribbon, burial flowers, quiet prayer
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Pasatsat is a Pangasinan ghost associated with tragic deaths and improper burial, especially in stories connected to the Japanese occupation during World War II. In ALAMAT, the Pasatsat is handled as a sorrowful war ghost rather than a monster to defeat. It appears as a reed mat blocking a lonely road, carrying the smell of unacknowledged death and the pain of a name never properly spoken. The player must research old records, speak to descendants, open the spectral mat with respect, and perform rites that return the dead to memory. Once bonded, Pasatsat becomes a controller who uses fear, despair, root effects, and ancestral warnings, while also cleansing allies who are overwhelmed by terror.
monsters.folklore_summary: Pasatsat is described in Pangasinan ghost stories as the ghost of someone who died tragically, especially during World War II, and was buried in a reed mat instead of a proper coffin. It may appear to passersby and is associated with a foul smell and unresolved burial.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Pasatsat as a tragic memory ghost whose haunting demands recognition, proper burial, and historical remembrance.
monsters.physical_description: A floating rolled reed mat bound with pale cloth strips, with faint human-shaped mist inside, dim blue lights where eyes might be, and old road dust drifting around it. The design avoids visible gore.
monsters.weaknesses: Proper burial rites, remembered names, Sun after rain, clean candles, historical truth
monsters.resistances: Underworld, Ancestor, Fear, Despair, Silence, mist concealment
monsters.npc_related: Apo Samad the grave keeper; Elena the descendant; Captain Isko the road patrolman
monsters.boss_variant: Pasatsat ng Limang Daan — blocks multiple routes with grief echoes and road illusions.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Pasatsat na Walang Pangalan — spreads Despair and erases quest clues if ignored.
monsters.divine_variant: Kaluluwang Naipahinga — rested war ghost wrapped in clean white light.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A muffled voice from inside a reed mat, followed by a sorrowful road-wind sigh.
monsters.drop_items: Reed Mat Fiber, War Memory Shard, White Candle Wax, Name Ribbon
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Bone Talisman, Ancestral Charm, Bell, Anting-Anting, Woven Garb
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Pasatsat inspired by Pangasinan ghost folklore: floating rolled reed mat ghost, pale cloth bindings, faint human mist silhouette inside, blue ghost lights, road dust aura, no gore, transparent background, readable chibi RPG spirit silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Pasatsat, Pangasinan reed-mat ghost connected to tragic wartime burial, rolled mat with misty human presence, lonely foggy road, candles and name ribbon, respectful somber RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: a reed mat unrolls across the battlefield like a road barrier, blue mist rises into old silhouettes, enemies are rooted by mat fibers, and grief echoes pulse outward.
monsters.design_notes: Verified folklore basis. Treat World War II death themes respectfully; focus on remembrance and burial dignity.

monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Eastern Visayan folklore and related regional tree-spirit traditions
monsters.passive_ability: Santol Tree Watch — Gains Defense in tree habitats. If attacked while Guarding, may counter with Shadow/Forest damage.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Rattan Cane Warning — Strikes the ground with a cane, taunting enemies and lowering their Attack.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Tree-Dweller's Misfortune — Calls dark leaves and root shadows around enemies, applying Weakened and Blind.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Guard, Haste, Spirit Ward, Counter Ready, Reveal, Barrier, Calm, Blessing, Fear Cry, Quick Strike, Protect Ally, Fortify, Cleanse, Mark Prey, Regeneration
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires respecting the tree home and undoing misfortune caused by offense
monsters.habitat: Balete Grove, Bamboo Thicket, Mangrove Marsh, Ancestral House, Corrupted Wilds
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Santol fruit, tobacco leaf, rattan charm, clean water, quiet apology
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Agta is a large tree-dwelling spirit of Eastern Visayan folklore, often associated with dark complexion, great height, and trees such as santol or balete. In ALAMAT, Agta are older cousins of the tree-giant tradition: quieter, less theatrical than Kapre, and more closely tied to misfortune that follows disrespect. This Agta appears beside a santol tree after a road crew cuts roots without permission and laughs at local warnings. Accidents begin, tools break, and shadows gather under the tree. To earn trust, the player must restore the damaged roots, return a rattan cane charm, and teach the road crew the proper apology. Once bonded, Agta becomes a slow guardian who uses taunt, shadow leaves, and protective tree wards.
monsters.folklore_summary: Agta is described in Eastern Visayan folklore as a large dark tree-dwelling spirit found in places such as Samar, Leyte, Masbate, and Bohol. Accounts connect it to trees, swampy places, misfortune, and sometimes a cane or cigar-like traits.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Agta as an older regional tree-dweller whose quiet warnings protect tree homes and punish careless destruction.
monsters.physical_description: A very tall dark tree spirit with broad shoulders, bark-like skin, heavy brows, glowing amber eyes, a rattan cane, moss on the arms, and a cloak of santol leaves and tree shadow.
monsters.weaknesses: Respectful apology rites, restored roots, Sun purification when corrupted, mercury charm references, fire damage
monsters.resistances: Forest, Shadow, Earth, Fear, Taunt, physical damage, root terrain
monsters.npc_related: Mang Doro the road worker; Apo Luntian the tree elder; Tala the forest child
monsters.boss_variant: Agta ng Matandang Santol — controls root-shadow wards and misfortune counters.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Agta ng Putol na Ugat — root-wounded giant who spreads bad-luck zones.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Punong May Alaala — cleansed tree guardian with golden leaf-shadow aura.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A low tree-creak voice followed by a rattan cane tap and rustling santol leaves.
monsters.drop_items: Santol Shadow Leaf, Rattan Cane Chip, Agta Bark, Misfortune Root
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Totem, Pipe Relic, Woven Garb, Anting-Anting, Herbal Bundle
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Agta inspired by Eastern Visayan folklore: very tall dark tree-dwelling giant spirit, bark-like skin, broad shoulders, amber eyes, rattan cane, mossy arms, santol leaf cloak, transparent background, readable chibi RPG guardian silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Agta, Eastern Visayan tree-dwelling spirit, tall dark giant under santol and balete trees, rattan cane, amber eyes, moss and shadows, respectful RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Agta taps a rattan cane on the ground, santol leaves darken the field, root-shadows rise around enemies, and a misfortune glyph lowers their attack.
monsters.design_notes: Verified folklore basis. Keep distinct from Kapre: Agta is quieter, older, and more tied to Eastern Visayan tree-dweller traditions.
